summary: Editor randomly freezes leading to crashes

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.5 and 4.5.1

### System information

Windows 11 - intel i5 13600kf - nvidia rtx 4070

### Issue description

I don't have much to figure this out other than it happens a lot.

The editor randomly crashes during various operations.
Examples:
1. When I try to export a project I open the file popup to select where:
1. a) When I try to create a new directory it just sometimes stops responding and eventually crashes or I have to force kill it.
1. b) When I try to sort directories (I go through the various sorting options couple times since they don't often work) couple times then again it freezes and eventually crashes.
2. When I play my project fromthe editor (pop up game window) and move around with the camera sometimes it just freezes and then crashes.
3. When I play my project fromthe editor (pop up game window) and try to change some values in the editor (nothing in particular - shader values, color, Node3D transforms, node visibility) to see them real time in the game window. Sometimes it works couple times and then on the third change or so it just freezes the entire editor making it unresponsive, windows gives the white overlay over the godot editor and the spinning wheel and again as above it leads to a crash.
4. When I play the project from the editor (pop up game window) and then in the editor I try to change the Albedo value of my mesh, I open the color picker and then click on the eyedrop icon to select a color, move it around and at some point it again freezes and then crashes.
5. Switching scene tabs in the editor leads to freeze + crash as above
6. Trying to create new directories in the FileSystem doc - sometimes it works, sometimes it again freezes everything and then crashes,
7. When playing the project, moving an object around in the editor to see the change realtime -> freeze -> crash.
8. And more.It's not connected to a particular project of mine, I tried creating a new scene and start working there with completely new stuff just to see and again freeze -> crashes.
9. And more.

I don't have any particular minimal repro project since I don't know how it happens, it's not my projects per se. I've also found the issues happening on mac and linux with other users when we talked about it.

Since it's random I don't know how to figure it out, I run the project from the console, I tried verbose but so far haven't had any luck.

There's one thing I can say for certain, it started after I moved to 4.5 (then ocntinued with 4.5.1), I did not have such issues in 4.4 as far as I can tell - of course sometimes godot crashed but nothing like this.

I can now work for couple hours fine and then start getting crashes several times in a row, or work for two minutes and agian crashes, for example I tried to export dialog as described above trying to sort the directories (just that, not even exporting, just selecting a path) and it crashed 7 times in a row, I eventually made a new directories in windows explorer and quickly selected in godot since it was impossible to use.

### Steps to reproduce

I wish I had more but I don't. I don't know how to reproduce it, the randomness makes it difficult too. No particular project itself is related - old projects moved to 4.5 as well as new projects in 4.5 have the same issue.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I wish I had more but I don't. I don't know how to reproduce it, the randomness makes it difficult too. No particular project itself is related - old projects moved to 4.5 as well as new projects in 4.5 have the same issue.

summary: TileSet: Add option to pack multiple tile images into single atlas

description.text:
### Origin
I was using Kenney.nl's race texture pack but was a bit annoyed with the prospect of having to create my own unified atlas .png to properly use the TileSet. Instead, I've adjusted the code to allow for the importing of multiple image files, and an atlas is automatically created from there.

### Changes
- Added new menu option "Atlas from Multiple Images" in the TileSet sources add menu
- Implemented grid-based packing that ensured proper image alignment with Godot's grid-only coordinate system
- Uses first image dimensions as the grid cell size (works for my purpose but probably needs adjusting for images of multiple sizes before going to main)

### Testing
Tested manually by importing multiple tile images of the same size and verifying proper atlas functionality. 

Thanks all!

summary: Fix autoset of `text_editor/external/exec_flags` not working properly

description.text:
Fixes an issue with #98846 not getting the paths correctly, especially PATH commands.

The current way seems to RegEx the entire path, including the path leading to the binary and the extension, just to find a pattern in the binary name. It doesn't seem to detect anything unless it starts with a slash.

My change uses `get_file().get_basename()`, with the RegEx being used solely to find a valid binary name. This version merely tries to read whatever name is in the Exec Path field and match, no need for slashes.

If the resulting file has "code", it will be treated as Visual Studio Code. "subl" will be treated as Sublime Text, "rider" will be treated as JetBrains Rider, etc.

Before:
1. Open editor settings
2. At External Editor, set Exec Path to "code" or "code.exe"
3. Nothing else happens (a.k.a. not working)
4. Set Exec Path to "/usr/bin/kate"
5. Exec Flags will change to `{file} --line {line} --column {col}` (working).

After:
1. Open editor settings
2. At External Editor, set Exec Path to "code" or "code.exe"
3. Exec Flags will change to `{project} --goto {file}:{line}:{col}` (working)
4. Set Exec Path to "/usr/bin/kate"
5. Exec Flags will change to `{file} --line {line} --column {col}` (still working)

I found this while trying to make #111821. I didn't even know this feature was in until I read the documentation.

summary: Fix switch to GameView when closing game window

description.text:
- *Fixes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/110840*

## Issue Description

Godot added a function to jump to GameView for debugging at Dec 30, 2024.

- https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/100916
- https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/100902

This feature add a timer into `embedded_process` to watch whether the `EDITOR_GAME` focused. This time will be triggered each `0.1 second`.

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/7864ac80192e9e91bf56176af9f04cc013b580aa/editor/run/game_view_plugin.cpp#L497-L501

It will switch to `EDITOR_GAME` when `get_window_enabled() == false`.

When we close testing game window, it will trigger `_window_close_request()`.

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/7864ac80192e9e91bf56176af9f04cc013b580aa/editor/run/game_view_plugin.cpp#L1071-L1092

At first, we invoke `set_window_enabled(false)`, then consider to invoke `embedded_process.reset()`. Unfortunately, when the condition doesnot match, e.g. `is_embedding_completed`, we wont `reset` `embedded_process` immediately. That  makes the timer keep alive for a short time.

If the timer success trigger a signal in that short time, it will find `get_window_enabled()` is false, and control editor switch to `EDITOR_GAME`. 

So, invoke `reset()` before `set_enabled(false)` can solve this issue.

summary: When opening an inherited scene in the editor, its local-to-scene resources get duplicated and saved as overrides

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.3, 4.4, 4.5

### System information

Godot v4.5.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1660 SUPER (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7652) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10700F CPU @ 2.90GHz (16 threads) - 15.92 GiB memory

### Issue description

When opening an inherited scene in the editor, any non-overridden resources on it marked as `Local to Scene` are duplicated and saved as overrides. This is problematic because:
- An override is created despite the user never expressing an intent to override
- The scene becomes unlinked from any edits to the original resource, leaving the user no way to make edits that propagate, which is the whole point of inheriting scenes.
- The inherited scene now stores an additional resource, which can be very large depending on the resource type

This is related to #94531 but it is not exactly the same, because the issue is more general than reported there. Additionally, it doesn't actually matter if the original resource is imported or if it's embedded in the base scene. Either way, the inherited scene will duplicate it and save it as an override.

### Steps to reproduce

- Open `box.tscn` in the attached project.
- Observe that the box has `box.material` assigned as its material.
- Open `inherited_box.tscn`.
- Observe that the box has an overridden, duplicated material.
- Clear the override, so that it reverts back to using `box.material`.
- Save and reload the scene.
- Observe that the box now once again has the overridden, duplicated material.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[bug-report-2.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22987383/bug-report-2.zip)

summary: MenuButton popup not affected by shader when rendered inside a SubViewport

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: Godot_v4.5.1-stable_win64

### System information

Godot v4.5.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5060 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7703) - AMD Ryzen AI 7 350 w/ Radeon 860M (16 threads) - 15.02 Gio memory

### Issue description

When rendering a UI inside a `SubViewport`, applying a full-screen shader to the `SubViewport`’s texture does not affect `MenuButton` popups.

If the same shader is applied to the main viewport, the popup is correctly affected.
This breaks visual consistency when using post-processing effects like CRT distortion, screen curvature, or color grading inside an in-world screen (e.g., a PDA, monitor, or helmet HUD).

It seems that the `MenuButton` popup is still drawn by the root viewport, not the `SubViewport`, so it bypasses the shader entirely.

**Expected behavior**

All UI elements rendered through a `SubViewport`, including popup menus, should be affected by the `SubViewport`’s shader.

**Actual behavior**

The `MenuButton` popup is drawn on top of everything by the main viewport and is not affected by the `SubViewport` shader.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a `SubViewport` and render it inside a `TextureRect`.
2. Push input from the `TextureRect` to the `SubViewport`.
3. Inside the `SubViewport`:
    - Add a `Control` scene containing a `MenuButton`.
    - Add a `CanvasLayer` with layer 1025, and a `ColorRect` with a `ShaderMaterial` affecting the visuals (e.g. https://godotshaders.com/shader/vhs-and-crt-monitor-effect-2/)
4. Run the scene and open the `MenuButton` dropdown.
5. Observe that the popup menu is not affected by the shader.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[menubutton.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22986126/menubutton.zip)

summary: Add signals to scrollbar increment/decrement buttons

description.text:
## What
Add signals to scrollbar increment/decrement button press/release:
- `increment_pressed`
- `increment_released`
- `decrement_pressed`

I wanted to detect when these buttons were being held so that I could auto repeat scrolling on a scrollbar

summary: Combination of MultiMesh with ArrayMesh created without vertex array does only render for more than one instance

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.5.stable.official [876b29033], v4.1.3.stable.official [f06b6836a], v4.6.dev1.official [8d8041bd4]

### System information

Godot v4.5.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6800 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 31.0.14057.2005) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800X3D 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 63.92 GiB memory

### Issue description

A combination (see "Steps to reproduce") of MultiMesh, ArrayMesh created without vertices and ShaderMaterial does not render / does not update transform when only one instance is set.
It does not render anything on first invocation with 1 instance.
It does render 1 instance, if multimesh.instance_count was greater 1 and is then set to 1, but the rendering does not update its transformation when MultiMeshInstance3D is transformed.
It does update the rendering "in place", if other parameters/uniforms are changed.

### Steps to reproduce

Open the example project. The project sets up:
- MultiMeshInstance3D with multimesh set to a MultiMesh and material_override set to a ShaderMaterial.
- MultiMesh: transform_format is set to TRANSFORM_3D and mesh is an empty ArrayMesh.

The script creates indices and uses add_surface_from_arrays with ARRAY_FLAG_USES_EMPTY_VERTEX_ARRAY to create geometry in the ArrayMesh. Then it creates the required number of instances.

Finally, the ShaderMaterial will create the vertices.

If the projects starts up with 1 instance, nothing is shown. If you change the number of instances to be larger than 1, everything works as expected. You can move/rotate the MultiMeshInstance3D and it works like expected.

If you now reduce the number of instances to 1 again, one instance is shown. If you change any uniform, the change is visible. But if you move/rotate the MultiMeshInstance3D, the rendering does not follow.

It does work if you add an array of vertices to the add_surface_from_arrays call. The vertices don't even need to be at the correct positions.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[test-multi-mesh-array-mesh-shader.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22983560/test-multi-mesh-array-mesh-shader.zip)

summary: No funciona lapiz Cintiq 13

summary: Improve performance of SpinBox creation

description.text:
This affects all LineEdits (and TextEdits) where `set_emoji_menu_enabled()` is called, notably SpinBoxes and things that use SpinBoxes like ColorPicker and across the Editor.
The `set_emoji_menu_enabled()` method generated the context menu when it doesn't need to. `_update_context_menu()` is already called before the menu is opened.

The menu is still generated when the menu is opened. I have not noticed any performance issues when opening the menu. (It is the same case as when `set_emoji_menu_enabled()` is not called).

I could have checked if the menu exists instead of removing the update, but I doubt this setting ever needs to be called while the menu is already visible, and other settings that affect the menu like `editable` don't update it immediately either.

Also disabled the emoji menu from the ScriptEditor's GotoLinePopup LineEdit (it's for numbers and `:` only).

Tested by making 5000 Spinboxes (in a debug build):
```gdscript
func _test():
	print("starting test...")
	var startms := Time.get_ticks_msec()
	for i in 5000:
		var sb := SpinBox.new()
		add_child(sb)
	var endms := Time.get_ticks_msec()
	print("finished in %sms" % (endms - startms))
```

before - finished in 32787ms / 29885ms / 29500ms
after - finished in 3962ms / 4035ms / 4085ms
Which is ~7.6x faster.

summary: `SeparationRayShape2D` resolves penetration without accounting for motion

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in v4.5.1.stable.official [f62fdbde1] ### System information Godot v4.5.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8129) - AMD Ryzen 7 7745HX with Radeon Graphics (16 threads) - 63.18 GiB memory ### Issue description By default, `SeparationRayShape2D` disregards the motion of it's body when resolving collisions. This is due to `GodotSpace2D::test_body_motion` skipping step 2 (motion collision) for `SeparationRayShape2D`. This results in penetration only being resolved on the frame after it happens. In many cases this results in a single frame where the body that has the `SeparationRayShape2D` visibly sinking into walls/floors/etc. When a constant large velocity is applied to the body, this results in a constant penetration of the body. What seems to be happening is thus: 1. Frame starts 1. Body checks for collisions but finds none as `SeparationRayShape2D` doesn't check along motion 1. Body moves and now overlaps 1. Frame ends and displays body overlapping 1. Frame starts 1. Body checks for collisions and finds the overlap 1. Body resolves collision 1. Body moves and now overlaps again. 1. Frame ends and displays body overlapping The expected behavior it that the depenetration step should move the `SeparationRayShape2D` out of collision entirely regardless of velocity. While this is technically resolved by setting `slide_on_slope` to `true` for the `SeparationRayShape2D`, this behavior is not indicated in the documentation (plus it isn't intuitive). Incapsulates #77571 ### Steps to reproduce All that is necessary is to have a `CharacterBody2D` with a `SeparationRayShape2D`. It will be more apparent the larger the velocity and the more parallel the `SeparationRayShape2D` and collision normal are. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [example.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22973066/example.zip) Enable Debug -> Visible Collision Shapes Contains a `CharacterBody2D` plus a `RigidBody2D` for reference. Press space to apply a constant velocity to the `CharacterBody2D`

summary: Game View Plugin: Fix signal connected too early causing theme warning

description.text:
- *Fixed: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/111751*

**Issue Description**

When we set `Toggle Selection Visibility` in `GameView`, there will save a project setting, named `hide_selection=true` in `user://.godot/editor/project/project_metadata.cfg`.

When we restart editor, it will load `hide_selection` from project config, using this config to update button.

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/60710df3b6fffb5a9a9479f80a0e9d8f05069946/editor/run/game_view_plugin.cpp#L1208-L1210

As we can see, the button already bind an event listener `_hide_selection_toggled`, to response when button toggled. So the init operation will trigger `_hide_selection_toggled` in `L1210`.

Actually, we didn't want to trigger event at initiating time, the event will be triggered later on `NOTIFICATION_THEME_CHANGED`, so moved `connect` behind `set_press` can solve this issue.

summary: Key insertion at timeline cursor creates offset duplicates

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in godot 4.6.dev [25981be] and later

### System information

NixOS - Godot v4.6.dev 

### Issue description

With the “Insert at current time” toggle enabled (added in [PR #107511](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/107511)), inserting keyframes repeatedly at the same timeline position creates multiple keys with tiny time offsets (like 1.00, 1.01, 1.02…).

This happens because the insertion logic shifts the key slightly instead of overwriting the existing one. While this prevents data loss, it leads to clutter and isn’t what most users would expect.

Ideally, it should either:

* Overwrite the existing key at that position
or
* Skip insertion and show a warning or a message

Here’s a quick demo of the issue:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/08c9a019-a164-40da-9762-feb9783d3fcf


### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new AnimationPlayer.
2. Enable “Insert at current time”.
3. Move the timeline cursor to a specific time (e.g. 1.0).
4. Press the insert key multiple times.

You'll see multiple keyframes stacked very close together.

summary: Android: Supported devices reduced if changing from gl_compatibility to mobile renderer

description.text: ### Tested versions Reproducible in 4.5.stable, 4.5.1.stable ### System information N/A ### Issue description Exporting an Android project with the rendering method set to `mobile` leads to a significant drop in supported devices on Play Store. This is unexpected, since `mobile` according to documentation\* should fallback to `gl_compatibility` if not supported. Thus I would expect the number of supported devices to be equal or greater; not fewer. The only changes between the 2 versions in the above screenshot are: * `version_code` incremented by 1 (required to be unique per upload, does not affect supported devices) * `rendering/renderer/rendering_method.mobile` changed from `gl_compatibility` to `mobile`. Everything else is identical. The project has Gradle Build enabled, exporting to AAB, `armeabi-v7a` + `arm64-v8a`. \* Documentation: > Since Godot 4.4, when using Forward+ or Mobile, if Vulkan is not supported, the engine will fall back to Direct3D 12 and vice versa. If the attempted fallback driver is not supported either, the engine will then fall back to Compatibility when the RenderingDevice backend is not supported. This allows the project to run anyway, but it may look different than the intended appearance due to the more limited renderer. This behavior can be disabled in the project settings by unchecking [Rendering > Rendering Device > Fallback to OpenGL 3] \- https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/rendering/renderers.html If this **is** expected behavior, it would be beneficial to have a note in the documentation regarding this. ### Steps to reproduce Export and upload any project to Google Play Store, with rendering method set to `mobile` vs `gl_compatibility`. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

summary: [macOS] Add support for `Fn` key modifier.

description.text:
Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/111689

Notes:
- Only `Fn` key on the Apple keyboards is handled (can be also tested with macOS virtual keyboard), `Fn` on most of PC keyboards (including third-party keyboards designed for Macs, like Logitech MX) is not a real key and handled internally by keyboard itself.
- `Fn` combinations won't show in native menu, this seems to be macOS limitation (`NSEventModifierFlagFunction is only supported for system-provided menu items; will not be use` is logged in the system Console.app if you try to).
- `Fn` key only works in combination with some keys (letter and number keys, and not other).
- `Fn` works as modifier only and can't be used as standalone key.
- There are a lot of system defined combinations using `Fn`, so it should be used with caution.

PR marks unused modifier defines `KPAD` and `GROUP_SWITCH` as deprecated, and reuses one of the values for `FN`. So it should not be breaking.

summary: GDScript editor: Incorrect highlighting in certain cases (single-line for loops, multi-line func declarations)

description.text: ### Tested versions Versions i had on hand for testing: 3.5.1, 3.6, 4.1.2, 4.2.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.4.1, 4.5.0, 4.5.1. Wrong color within multiline func declaration: - Reproduced in all versions i tested. Wrong color in single-line for loop: - Reproduced in 4.3 and higher. - Not reproduced in 4.2.2 and lower. ### System information Windows 10 (build 19045) - Not tested on other platforms but no reason on earth to expect any difference ### Issue description There are a couple of cases where Godot's script editor gets the highlighting wrong. Namely the cases mentioned in the issue's title. In the case of a single-line for loop, it's that assigning a variable after the colon will color it "Base Type Color" green. It seems as if the highlighter wrongly thinks that it's a type as in the strong-typing pattern `var something: String = "hello"`. In the case of multi-line function declarations (that is, when within the parentheses to put each parameter on its own line), instead of being "Base Type Color" green like they are within a single-line declaration, type hints of inner classes take on the "Member Variable" blue. Built-in types and classes such as any derived from `Object` or `Node` are not affected by this. Custom script classes defined with `class_name` have the "User Type" color as expected. Not sure exactly what is happening there besides the highlighter possibly just not realizing it's within a function declaration. Naturally the fixes would be within [modules/gdscript/editor/gdscript_highlighter.cpp](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/36b92128b1bce1f98687add98c84fd70ae9b20ca/modules/gdscript/editor/gdscript_highlighter.cpp), but when i spent a few minutes on Sunday looking at the file i didn't really nail down where those fixes would have to take place. Lot going on in there. ### Steps to reproduce Open `script.gd` within the MRP in the Godot editor. Or open a blank project, create a new script and type or paste into it: ```gdscript class InnerClass: var data: Array[int] func foo( bar: InnerClass ): var data := bar.data for baz in data1: baz = baz + 3 ``` (Yes, i realize this code doesn't do what whoever would've written it apparently intended. Just the case to show the syntax highlighting in minimal lines.) Observe the highlighting: The parameter typing `InnerClass` should be green, and `baz` in line 8 should not. Note the putting of `bar.data` into a local variable, as when using `bar.data` directly to initiate the for loop the highlighting is as expected. The green `baz` could be anything, not just the loop variable, but it is in this case to keep the lines down. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [bad-hl-mrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22936127/bad-hl-mrp.zip)

summary: Fix incorrect selection drawing when transform changes during frame processing but is the same at the end of the frame

description.text:
Fixes #105564

Issue was that nested `Container`s may take an arbitrary amount of deferred sort calls to adjust positions and put its descendants in many intermediate positions before the final positions are set. If the editor viewport is redrawn between any of the Container sort calls, various parts (selection highlight in the original issue, but things like relative snapping rulers would also be affected) will be drawn using some incorrect intermediate position, but not be corrected by the transform and rect checks since there wasn't a change between values at the end of the frame.

The fix changes the redraw trigger for transform and rect changes to be comparing against the values at last draw rather than at last frame. This should fix any kind of mistimed drawing since it'll eventually get corrected by the process notification. 

`prev_drawn` was also added since the transform and rect values being are meaningless until a draw has occurred. Tangentially, it fixed a similar issue of not causing a redraw when selecting a control that was at (0,0) with size (0,0) that I realized while fixing.

This fixes a few cases where dict[] operator was being used when the key (or value?) didn't exist. See relevant PR that started to error print these cases: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/106636

I don't have a MRP at the moment, but can confirm that this fixes issues with my project getting lots of the `Bug: Dictionary::operator[] used when there was no value for the given key, please report.` spam when editing the project with VSCode while connected to Godot LSP.

Thanks @Ivorforce for the small nudge to figure this out myself.
